message:
feat(ui): refactor board view with adaptive column navigation

Redesign the Kanban board to only show and navigate between columns
that contain issues, improving usability when some status categories
are empty.

Key changes to BoardModel:
- Add activeColIdx slice tracking indices of non-empty columns
- focusedCol now indexes into activeColIdx, not raw column indices
- Add actualFocusedCol() helper to map focused position to real column
- updateActiveColumns() rebuilds navigation list after data changes

Navigation improvements:
- MoveLeft/MoveRight skip empty columns automatically
- All navigation methods (MoveUp/Down/ToTop/ToBottom/PageUp/PageDown)
  now use actualFocusedCol() for correct column targeting
- Selection sanitization in SetIssues() prevents out-of-bounds access

Visual enhancements to View():
- Dynamic column width calculation based on visible column count
- Columns expand to fill available width (min 28, max 60 chars)
- Rich card rendering with type icons, priority badges, metadata row
- Scroll indicators when column content exceeds visible area
- Empty columns show "(empty)" placeholder with italic styling

Test coverage added:
- TestAdaptiveColumns: verifies empty column skipping during navigation
- TestColumnNavigation: up/down movement within columns
- TestPageNavigation: PageUp/PageDown bounds checking
- TestColumnCounts: count accessor methods
- TestSetIssuesSanitizesSelection: selection clamping after data update
- TestAllColumnsEmpty: empty board edge case handling
- TestSortingByPriorityAndDate: issue ordering verification
- TestViewRendering: rendering with various dimensions/inputs

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
